title_ko: 헷갈리는 한국어 모음, 이제 정확히 들려요!
title_en: Confusing Korean Vowels, Now You Can Hear Them Clearly!

scenes.ko: 안녕하세요, 졸라걸입니다! 오늘은 한국어 듣기 실력을 높여줄 아주 중요한 수업을 할 거예요.
scenes.en: Hello, I'm Zollanyeo! Today, we're going to have a very important lesson to improve your Korean listening skills.
scenes.bg_prompt: soft pastel storybook Korean folk village (hanok, tiled roofs, stone wall, clay jars) with a welcoming atmosphere and a clear path, thick friendly outlines, flat colors, no text, no people, leave the left third and lower area open, 16:9

scenes.ko: 한국어에는 발음이 비슷해서 헷갈리기 쉬운 모음들이 있어요. 이 모음들을 정확하게 구별하는 방법을 알려드릴게요.
scenes.en: In Korean, there are vowels that sound similar and are easy to confuse. I'll teach you how to distinguish these vowels accurately.
scenes.bg_prompt: soft pastel storybook Korean folk village (hanok, tiled roofs, stone wall, clay jars) with question marks gently floating around, thick friendly outlines, flat colors, no text, no people, leave the left third and lower area open, 16:9

scenes.ko: 첫 번째 짝은 'ㅗ'와 'ㅜ'입니다. 'ㅗ'는 입술을 둥글게 모아서 '오' 소리를 내고, 'ㅜ'는 입술을 더 내밀어서 '우' 소리를 내요. '오리'와 '우유'로 연습해 볼까요?
scenes.en: The first pair is 'ㅗ' and 'ㅜ'. For 'ㅗ', round your lips to make an 'o' sound. For 'ㅜ', push your lips out further to make a 'u' sound. Shall we practice with '오리' (duck) and '우유' (milk)?
scenes.bg_prompt: soft pastel storybook Korean folk village (hanok, tiled roofs, stone wall, clay jars) with a cute duck swimming in a peaceful pond and a carton of milk on a small wooden table, thick friendly outlines, flat colors, no text, no people, leave the left third and lower area open, 16:9

scenes.ko: 다음은 'ㅐ'와 'ㅔ'입니다. 'ㅐ'는 입을 크게 벌리고 '애' 소리를, 'ㅔ'는 입을 조금만 벌려 '에' 소리를 내요. '개'와 '게'를 들어보세요.
scenes.en: Next are 'ㅐ' and 'ㅔ'. For 'ㅐ', open your mouth wide for an 'ae' sound. For 'ㅔ', open your mouth slightly for an 'e' sound. Listen to '개' (dog) and '게' (crab).
scenes.bg_prompt: soft pastel storybook Korean folk village (hanok, tiled roofs, stone wall, clay jars) with a friendly dog near a traditional gate and a crab walking on a sunny stone path, thick friendly outlines, flat colors, no text, no people, leave the left third and lower area open, 16:9

scenes.ko: 이번엔 'ㅏ'와 'ㅓ'입니다. 'ㅏ'는 입을 크게 벌리고 '아' 소리를, 'ㅓ'는 입을 조금만 벌리고 턱을 내린 채 '어' 소리를 내요. '말'과 '머리'로 구분해 볼까요?
scenes.en: This time, it's 'ㅏ' and 'ㅓ'. For 'ㅏ', open your mouth wide for an 'a' sound. For 'ㅓ', open your mouth slightly and lower your jaw for an 'eo' sound. Can you distinguish '말' (horse) and '머리' (head)?
scenes.bg_prompt: soft pastel storybook Korean folk village (hanok, tiled roofs, stone wall, clay jars) with a gentle horse grazing in a green field and a simple traditional statue of a person's head on a stone pedestal, thick friendly outlines, flat colors, no text, no people, leave the left third and lower area open, 16:9

scenes.ko: 마지막으로 'ㅡ'와 'ㅜ'입니다. 'ㅡ'는 입술을 옆으로 살짝 당겨서 '으' 소리를, 'ㅜ'는 입술을 둥글게 내밀어 '우' 소리를 내요. '불'과 '뿔'로 연습해 보세요.
scenes.en: Finally, 'ㅡ' and 'ㅜ'. For 'ㅡ', pull your lips slightly to the sides to make an 'eu' sound. For 'ㅜ', round and push your lips out to make a 'u' sound. Practice with '불' (fire) and '뿔' (horn).
scenes.bg_prompt: soft pastel storybook Korean folk village (hanok, tiled roofs, stone wall, clay jars) with a small, contained campfire burning and a drawing of an animal with majestic horns on a traditional wall, thick friendly outlines, flat colors, no text, no people, leave the left third and lower area open, 16:9

scenes.ko: '오'와 '우'는 입술 모양, '애'와 '에'는 입의 크기, '아'와 '어'는 턱의 위치, '으'와 '우'는 입술의 당김이 중요했어요.
scenes.en: For 'ㅗ' and 'ㅜ', lip shape was key. For 'ㅐ' and 'ㅔ', mouth size. For 'ㅏ' and 'ㅓ', jaw position. For 'ㅡ' and 'ㅜ', lip tension was important.
scenes.bg_prompt: soft pastel storybook Korean folk village (hanok, tiled roofs, stone wall, clay jars) with various abstract diagrams of Korean vowel mouth shapes for pronunciation, thick friendly outlines, flat colors, no text, no people, leave the left third and lower area open, 16:9

scenes.ko: 잘하셨어요! 이제 각 모음 짝들의 미묘한 차이를 잘 들을 수 있겠죠? 반복해서 들으며 익숙해지는 것이 중요해요.
scenes.en: Well done! Now you should be able to hear the subtle differences in each vowel pair, right? It's important to get used to them by listening repeatedly.
scenes.bg_prompt: soft pastel storybook Korean folk village (hanok, tiled roofs, stone wall, clay jars) with a peaceful study area featuring Korean books and a warm cup of tea, thick friendly outlines, flat colors, no text, no people, leave the left third and lower area open, 16:9

scenes.ko: 꾸준히 연습하면 한국어 듣기 실력이 눈에 띄게 향상될 거예요. 다음 시간에도 유익한 한국어 수업으로 찾아올게요! 안녕!
scenes.en: With consistent practice, your Korean listening skills will improve significantly. See you next time for another beneficial Korean lesson! Bye!
scenes.bg_prompt: soft pastel storybook Korean folk village (hanok, tiled roofs, stone wall, clay jars) with a joyful and bright atmosphere and a beautiful sunset, thick friendly outlines, flat colors, no text, no people, leave the left third and lower area open, 16:9
